turns.content: Why does this even work this way? I read somewhere that maybe you need to call it twice.
I get the feeling that something feels off; let's unpack it together. What part seems unclear?
Because calling a function twice would definitely make things work, right?
Actually, calling a function more than once usually doesn’t change its behavior; it’s not like executing code twice magically fixes the issue.
I think it’s just a lazy shortcut people use instead of proper fixes.
That’s one perspective — some see it as a temporary measure to get things running while they figure out a better solution later.
If it works now, why bother making it better? The app won’t break anytime soon.
I understand the desire for stability. However, small changes can prevent bigger problems down the line without much risk.
It feels scary to touch code later if I’m not sure what will happen.
That’s a common worry; many developers start with simple solutions and gradually add robustness as they become comfortable.
